path.markdownDescription:
Location of the output file

Specifies a path on disk to write [TenXObject](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXObject) instance and template field values.

append.markdownDescription:
Overwrite or append to file

Controls whether to overwrite the output file or append TenXObject instance/template field values to its end. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

header.markdownDescription:
Header value to write to file

Specifies a header file to write to file in [outputFileAppend](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfileappend) is false.

writeObjects.markdownDescription:
If true, encode TenXObjects [outputFileFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfilefields) to this output

Determine whether to write [outputFileFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfilefields) values of an TenXObject passed to this output. This configuration is typically defined using a JavaScript expression. To enable/disable the output depending on whether a launch argument, environment variable, or JVM -D option evaluates as truthy, use: ``` yaml   outputFileWriteObjects: $=TenXEnv.get("myFilePath") ``` To learn more see: [TenXEnv.get](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXEnv.get). (Default: true)

filter.markdownDescription:
A JavaScript expression an TenXObject must evaluate as 'truthy' against to be written to file

Specifies a JavaScript expression that an TenXObject must evaluate as truthy to write its instance/template field values to this output. For example, 'outputFileFilter: this.timestamp' will only emit [timestamped](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+timestamped) TenXObjects.

fields.markdownDescription:
List of TenXObject field names to encode

Specifies a list of TenXObject intrinsic/calculated/extracted field names to extract and write to the output.

type.markdownDescription:
The output format in which to encode TenXObject values. Possible values:[json, delimited]

Specifies how [outputFileFields](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfilefields) are encoded to a stream/event to this output. Possible values: - **json**: formats names and values for the current TenXObject as: {"field":"str","field2":1, "field3": true} - **delimited**: formats values for the current TenXObject as: "str",1,true    The [outputFileEncodeDelimiter](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfileencodedelimiter) argument determines the separator to use. (Default: delimited)

delimiter.markdownDescription:
The delimiter placed between key and values pairs if 'outputFileEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the delimiter to separate TenXObject field values when [outputFileEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfileencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default: ,)

groupDelimiter.markdownDescription:
String delimiter to use when separating individual 10x events inside a group

Sets the string delimiter used to separate individual 10x events inside a group when encoded to a stream/event output

encodedLinePrefix.markdownDescription:
The line prefix prepended to the encoded event if 'outputFileEncodeType' is 'delimited'

Sets the char which is prepended to the emitted TenXObject field values when [outputFileEncodeType](https://doc.log10x.com/run/output/event/file/#outputfileencodetype) is `delimited`. (Default: ~)

writeTemplates.markdownDescription:
If true, write new TenXTemplates to this output

Controls whether to write template values of TenXObjects passed to this output. The output will only emit TenXTemplates that the pipeline did not load at startup via the [templateFile](https://doc.log10x.com/run/template/#templatefiles) argument, and will only emit an TenXTemplate once based on its [templateHash](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+templateHash) value. (Default: false)

log4j2.markdownDescription:
If true, write using log4j2 appender

Controls whether to write to file using an [OutputStream](https://doc.log10x.com/api/output/#outputstream) or a [log4j2 appender](https://doc.log10x.com/api/output/#log4j2). (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: false)
